text: Managing a team effectively requires more than just giving orders. Clear communication is the foundation—everyone must understand their tasks and the project goals. Delegation is equally important; assigning tasks based on each member's strengths builds trust and efficiency. Regular feedback, both positive and constructive, helps team members improve and feel valued. To keep motivation high, acknowledge hard work and celebrate small wins. Conflict is inevitable, but it can be resolved calmly by listening to all sides and finding common ground. By balancing these elements, you create a supportive environment where the team can thrive.
